hero.structure: holiday-contrast recap (screenshot line 1, receipts middle, provocation close)
hero.text:
America took a long weekend. The wallets we track didn't.

Friday through Sunday: 22-14, +23 units.

Both 6-unit max plays cashed — Colombia, France ✅✅
England +155, the dog the winning money wouldn't let go of ✅
MLB: 18-11, green all three days

That's +54 units since June 1. Every pick posted before lock. Every L still on the page.

New here? We don't cap games. We track a small circle of bettors who've won for years — and post exactly where their money goes, every day.

Bookmark this. Grade us all week. Then come back and tell me it's luck.
hero.rtLine: America took a long weekend. The wallets we track didn't.
hero.postWindow: 11:30 AM - 1:00 PM ET Monday (back-to-work scroll, biggest recap audience of the week)
hero.selfReplyAt25min:
The part nobody believes: we were RED on Wednesday (-7u) and posted it anyway. Then Friday went 8-4 (+16u) and the weekend never looked back.

The Ls are why the Ws count. Full tracker, every graded pick: nhlsavant.com

alternates.text:
We made +23 units this weekend and I watched exactly zero of the games.

Grilled Friday. Lake Saturday. Fireworks Sunday.

The wallets we track handled the rest: 22-14 across MLB and the World Cup, both 6-unit max plays cashed, England +155 as the cherry on top.

+54 units since June 1st, posted before lock, Ls included.

Is betting without watching the games disrespectful to sports, or the only honest way to do it?

If you're seeing this account for the first time, stop scrolling for 30 seconds.

This weekend: 22-14, +23 units.
Since June 1: +54 units. Every single pick timestamped before lock, every loss left up.

No lock-of-the-year graphics. No DMs to buy anything. We track bettors who actually win and show you their money in real time.

Follow and bookmark — then hold us to it this week. That's the whole pitch.

rules: reply to every commenter in first 30 min
